channel.title: AI·Brief — Daily Tech Intelligence
channel.description: Automated AI-powered tech news briefings updated every 3 hours.

item.title: Framework Delays Laptop 13 Pro Shipments
item.description: Framework's Laptop 13 Pro shipments are delayed by a month due to issues with the haptic trackpad and custom display. New expected shipping is now in July.

item.title: Ubisoft Announces Studio Closures and Layoffs
item.description: Ubisoft has closed its Winnipeg and Belgrade studios amid a new restructuring, resulting in hundreds of layoffs. This move is part of a broader organizational change.

item.title: Intel’s Project Firefly Unveiled
item.description: Intel's Project Firefly aims to create affordable laptops with premium features, utilizing Wildcat Lake SoCs in a sleek, ventless design. Targeting budget-conscious consumers.

item.title: Amazon’s Echo Speakers Aid Kids' Sleep
item.description: Amazon introduces Sleep Studio, enhancing Echo and Echo Kids speakers to help children wind down and fall asleep with calming stories and sounds. Aimed at improving bedtime routines.

item.title: PlayStation Plus Adds 8 Free Games
item.description: PlayStation Plus subscribers can enjoy eight new free games this June, expanding their gaming library. Exciting titles are now available for download.

item.title: Nintendo Stock Drops After Direct
item.description: Nintendo's stock fell nearly 7% following a disappointing June Direct that lacked a new 3D Mario game, despite positive fan reactions. Investors remain cautious.

item.title: Street Fighter 6 DLC Reveals New Characters
item.description: Capcom reveals new details about Yasmine and Arjun, newcomers in Street Fighter 6's Season 4. Fans are excited about the latest updates.

item.title: Deltarune Chapter 5 Release Date Announced
item.description: Toby Fox announces the release date for Deltarune Chapter 5 and provides updates on Chapter 6, exciting fans of the series. Anticipation builds for the next installment.

item.title: EU Responds to Apple's Siri AI Decision
item.description: The European Commission states that Apple's choice not to launch Siri AI in Europe is solely Apple's decision, clarifying its stance on the matter. Apple sought legal exemptions.
